# Gdn Guardiankey

## Overview

gSage AI — GDN GuardianKey tool.

## Tool

| Tool | Summary | Permissions | Requires approval | Core |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | :---: |
| `gdn_guardiankey` | Query GuardianKey GDN analytics API for authentication events and risk-scoring data | `gdn:read` | — | — |

## Configuration

> The JSON below goes into the **configtool model** in the web UI (per organisation; can also be scoped to a department).

> **Multiple profiles supported** — you may create more than one config row per organisation, each with a distinct `profile_id`. The agent picks one via the `config_profile` parameter at call time. State, rate limits and circuit-breaker counters are isolated per profile.

> **Sensitive fields** (api keys / passwords / tokens) are *omitted* from the JSON below — set them via environment variables (see the _Environment variables_ table) or directly in the encrypted DB row from the web UI.

### Empty configtool template (required fields only)

```json
{}
```

### Example configtool model

```json
{
  "api_key": "",
  "gdn_org_id": "",
  "url": ""
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `api_key` | `string` | — | ✓ | `""` | GDN API key sent in the X-API-Key header. Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__API_KEY env var. |
| `gdn_org_id` | `string` | — | — | `""` | Organisation ID in the GDN platform (hex string). Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__GDN_ORG_ID env var. |
| `url` | `string` | — | — | `""` | GDN API base URL. The /api/v1 suffix is appended automatically if absent. Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__URL env var. |

## Environment variables

The variables below are derived automatically from each tool's `config_schema`. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) to seed defaults for every organisation. **Per-org DB config takes precedence over env vars.**

| Variable | Maps to field | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__API_KEY` | `api_key` | `string` | — | ✓ | GDN API key sent in the X-API-Key header. Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__API_KEY env var. |
| `TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__GDN_ORG_ID` | `gdn_org_id` | `string` | `""` | — | Organisation ID in the GDN platform (hex string). Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__GDN_ORG_ID env var. |
| `TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__URL` | `url` | `string` | `""` | — | GDN API base URL. The /api/v1 suffix is appended automatically if absent. Overrides TOOL_GDN_GUARDIANKEY__URL env var. |

## Operations

### `gdn_guardiankey`

- **`query`** — _(no description)_

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `report` | `string` | — | Report to query (required for action='query'). Available reports: top_users (top users by event count), top_clientips (top IPs by event count), events (detailed event list), users (user activity summary), top_clientips_users (IPs with distinct user counts), top_users_cities (users by distinct cities — impossible travel detection), areas_risk_treatment_in_time (risk timeline series), top_users_risk (users by cumulative risk score), pie_event_responses (response distribution), bars_events_in_time (event volume over time), table_top_countries (top countries), table_top_cities (top cities), table_top_threats (top threat categories), table_messagelog_events (WAP message log events). |

- **`list_reports`** — _(no description)_

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `report` | `string` | — | Report to query (required for action='query'). Available reports: top_users (top users by event count), top_clientips (top IPs by event count), events (detailed event list), users (user activity summary), top_clientips_users (IPs with distinct user counts), top_users_cities (users by distinct cities — impossible travel detection), areas_risk_treatment_in_time (risk timeline series), top_users_risk (users by cumulative risk score), pie_event_responses (response distribution), bars_events_in_time (event volume over time), table_top_countries (top countries), table_top_cities (top cities), table_top_threats (top threat categories), table_messagelog_events (WAP message log events). |
  | `days_ago` | `integer` | — | Number of days back from now to use as the time range start. Default: 7. Ignored if time_begin / time_end are provided. |
  | `time_begin` | `string` | — | Explicit time range start in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM). When set, takes priority over days_ago. |
  | `time_end` | `string` | — | Explicit time range end in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM). When set, takes priority over days_ago. |
  | `username` | `string` | — | Filter results by username. |
  | `client_ip` | `string` | — | Filter results by client IP address. |
  | `login_failed` | `boolean` | — | Filter by login outcome: true = failed logins only, false = successful logins only. |
  | `country` | `string` | — | Filter results by country name. |
  | `response` | `string` | — | Filter results by gSageKey treatment response. |

- **`usage_summary`** — _(no description)_

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `report` | `string` | — | Report to query (required for action='query'). Available reports: top_users (top users by event count), top_clientips (top IPs by event count), events (detailed event list), users (user activity summary), top_clientips_users (IPs with distinct user counts), top_users_cities (users by distinct cities — impossible travel detection), areas_risk_treatment_in_time (risk timeline series), top_users_risk (users by cumulative risk score), pie_event_responses (response distribution), bars_events_in_time (event volume over time), table_top_countries (top countries), table_top_cities (top cities), table_top_threats (top threat categories), table_messagelog_events (WAP message log events). |
  | `days_ago` | `integer` | — | Number of days back from now to use as the time range start. Default: 7. Ignored if time_begin / time_end are provided. |
  | `time_begin` | `string` | — | Explicit time range start in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM). When set, takes priority over days_ago. |
  | `time_end` | `string` | — | Explicit time range end in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM). When set, takes priority over days_ago. |
  | `username` | `string` | — | Filter results by username. |
  | `client_ip` | `string` | — | Filter results by client IP address. |
  | `login_failed` | `boolean` | — | Filter by login outcome: true = failed logins only, false = successful logins only. |
  | `country` | `string` | — | Filter results by country name. |
  | `response` | `string` | — | Filter results by gSageKey treatment response. |
